paper2.context:
SECTION A (Suggested time: 40 minutes)

You MUST answer this question.

Read the following article carefully and list FIVE MAIN points discussed, then write a summary of the article in NOT MORE THAN 120 words. If this limit is exceeded, only the first 120 words of your answer will be read and assessed.

As far as possible, use your own words. Your summary must be in continuous prose. Your summary MUST include the five points identified AND other relevant points.

Your answer will be assessed on how well you:
(a) identify the main ideas and opinions in the extract
(b) organise and express these ideas and opinions in your own words
(c) use appropriate grammar, sentence structure, vocabulary, spelling and punctuation.

— CELEBRITY RELATIONSHIPS —

The need to belong is a natural aspect of human development, forming the building blocks for our relationships and communities. The long-term effect of interaction with familiar persons allows us to deepen connections and develop intimate understanding. Such understanding is intimate because we care about, respond to and understand each other. In time, we surround ourselves with people who share our lives — partner, child, aunt, neighbour, employer. We trust them because we have shared experiences; we know who and what they are, they know us, and we are comfortable in their company.

There is a marked difference between the intimacy described above and the hype of relationships we have with celebrities. The explosion of the Internet and cable TV has created a difference in the way we understand people. We are now exposed to the celebrity in different stages. The difference is that there is no give-and-take or growth in such relationships. The celebrities remain figments of our imagination, beings whose lives seem exciting and glamorous, who awaken our curiosity, feed off the attention we give, and use it as currency to aid in accumulating fame and fortune.

In the world of 'popular' or 'celebrity' culture, there is no intimate connection. It is essentially a one-way communication, where the focus is lavished on the celebrity. The relationship created with the public is a deliberate strategy, manufactured, marketed and promoted. Celebrity today is about the brand, endorsements and glamorous lifestyles. The public and media interest is crucial, providing the material which the person needs to become a celebrity. Through sustained media focus, we are influenced to care about them, to support them on their way up, and to help them to maintain their positions once they reach the top.

In return, the celebrity provides most persons with an escape from their mundane lives and satisfies their curiosity about persons whose lives have been materially changed beyond their imagination. The celebrity has a public image to maintain, an image which is retouched and photoshopped until it achieves the desired level of perfection, and one that has virtually no bearing on the reality in the world we live in.

— Leah Longville, 'Celebrity: The New Role Model', In NEX Generation, No. 4, 2011

parts.question:
List FIVE MAIN points discussed in the article. You MUST use complete sentences.

1. _______________
2. _______________
3. _______________
4. _______________
5. _______________

rubric.value: real relationships are built on mutual understanding shared experiences and trust over time
celebrity relationships are one-way there is no genuine give-and-take or personal growth
celebrity culture is deliberately manufactured marketed and promoted as a strategy
celebrities use public attention as currency to build fame and fortune
celebrities provide an escape from ordinary life but their image is artificial and unrealistic

parts.question: Write a summary of the article in NOT MORE THAN 120 words. Write in continuous prose using your own words as far as possible.

rubric.value: CONTENT: includes main points — real vs celebrity relationships one-way nature of celebrity culture manufactured image escape from mundane life
LANGUAGE: own words used effectively not merely copied from the passage
ORGANISATION: continuous prose coherent paragraphing logical flow within word limit

paper2.context:
SECTION B (Suggested time: 30 minutes)

You MUST answer this question.

Study the situation presented below and then complete the task that follows.

John Mason, a 16-year-old, wants to find out what must be done in order to obtain his driver's licence. He called the licensing office many times but was unable to reach anyone. He decides to write an email using the email address he obtained. He must write to Mr Kenneth Davy, Head of the Traffic Department of the Royal St Taba Police Force. He wants the following information:

• The minimum age to acquire a driver's licence
• The tests (theory and practical) which must be taken
• The length of time it takes to get the licence after passing the test
• The days the tests are taken
• The cost of the driver's licence

Write the email using the information given. Make sure to include all the relevant details in the email to ensure that all the information is received.

Your email MUST be in continuous prose.

Your answer will be assessed on how well you:
• format the email
• select relevant and complete information
• organise and express the information
• use appropriate grammar, sentence structure, vocabulary, spelling and punctuation.

parts.question: Write John's email to Mr Kenneth Davy, Head of the Traffic Department.

rubric.value: FORMAT: To/From/Subject line correct email header Dear Mr Davy opening greeting appropriate closing e.g. Yours sincerely and name
CONTENT: all five required points included — minimum age, theory and practical tests, time to receive licence, days tests are held, cost
LANGUAGE: formal register appropriate for official email correct grammar Standard English
ORGANISATION: continuous prose logical paragraph structure coherent flow of information

paper2.context:
SECTION A (Suggested time: 40 minutes)

You MUST answer this question.

Read the following article carefully and list FIVE MAIN points discussed, then write a summary of the article in NOT MORE THAN 120 words.

As far as possible, use your own words. Your summary MUST include the five points AND other relevant points. It must be in continuous prose.

— SOCIAL MEDIA AND CARIBBEAN YOUTH —

Social media platforms have become deeply embedded in the daily lives of Caribbean young people. For many teenagers, these platforms are the primary means of communication, entertainment and self-expression. The ease of access through smartphones has meant that young people are online for hours each day, often at the expense of face-to-face interaction and academic focus.

Research conducted in several Caribbean territories indicates that prolonged social media use is associated with increased levels of anxiety, depression and low self-esteem, particularly among girls. The constant exposure to curated images of beauty, success and wealth creates unrealistic standards that many young people feel pressured to meet. Cyberbullying, which is widespread on these platforms, has also been linked to serious psychological harm, including self-harm and suicidal ideation among vulnerable teenagers.

However, social media also presents significant opportunities for Caribbean youth. Young entrepreneurs have successfully used platforms like Instagram and Facebook to build businesses and reach customers regionally and globally. Social media has also enabled Caribbean young people to celebrate their culture, amplify their voices on social issues and connect with the diaspora in ways that were not previously possible. Youth-led movements for environmental justice, gender equality and improved governance have all been driven significantly through social media campaigns.

The challenge, therefore, is not to simply ban social media use among young people but to equip them with the critical digital literacy skills that allow them to harness its benefits while protecting themselves from its harms. Parents, educators and policymakers all have a role to play in creating a healthier digital culture for Caribbean youth.

— Source: Adapted from a regional youth health study

the solution is not to ban social media but to teach young people critical digital literacy skills

rubric.value: CONTENT: covers both negative effects and positive opportunities concludes with need for digital literacy

ORGANISATION: continuous prose logical and coherent within the word limit

The Student Council at Green Valley Secondary School wishes to lobby the Principal to install a computer laboratory upgrade. As President of the Student Council, you have been asked to write a formal letter to the Principal, Mr David Charles, requesting the following:

• New computers to replace the outdated ones currently in use
• Reliable high-speed internet access in all classrooms
• Training for teachers on the use of new educational technology
• A dedicated IT support technician
• Extended library opening hours to allow access to technology after school

Write the formal letter. Make sure to include all relevant points and present them persuasively.

Your letter MUST be in continuous prose.

Your answer will be assessed on how well you:
• format the letter correctly
• select relevant and complete information
• organise and express the information persuasively
• use appropriate grammar, sentence structure, vocabulary, spelling and punctuation.

parts.question: Write the formal letter to Principal Mr David Charles.

rubric.value: FORMAT: sender address date recipient address correct salutation Dear Mr Charles subject line appropriate closing Yours sincerely and name
CONTENT: all five points addressed new computers high-speed internet teacher training IT technician extended library hours
PERSUASION: reasons given for each request presented as benefits to students and school
LANGUAGE: formal persuasive register correct Standard English grammar

paper2.context:
SECTION A (Suggested time: 40 minutes)

You MUST answer this question.

Read the following article carefully and list FIVE MAIN points discussed, then write a summary in NOT MORE THAN 120 words.

Your summary must be in continuous prose using your own words as far as possible.

— FOOD SECURITY IN THE CARIBBEAN —

Food security — the ability of all people to access sufficient, safe and nutritious food — remains a pressing challenge for Caribbean nations. Despite being surrounded by fertile land and abundant seas, many Caribbean countries import more than 60 percent of the food they consume. This heavy dependence on food imports makes the region extremely vulnerable to disruptions in global supply chains, price fluctuations and natural disasters.

The Caribbean's agricultural sector has declined significantly over recent decades. Young people have largely abandoned farming, viewing it as a low-status, low-income occupation with poor prospects. Meanwhile, traditional crops that once sustained communities have given way to cheaper, imported processed foods that are high in sugar, salt and fat. The result has been a dramatic rise in non-communicable diseases such as obesity, diabetes and hypertension across the region.

Research consistently shows that Caribbean countries have the capacity to feed themselves if the right investments are made. The region has suitable land, water resources and a tradition of agricultural knowledge. What is needed is political will, financial investment and a change in how farming is perceived — from a backward occupation to a modern, technology-driven and profitable enterprise. Several countries have begun to see success with initiatives promoting agri-business, hydroponic farming and farm-to-table supply chains.

Regional integration bodies such as CARICOM have recognised food security as a priority and set targets for reducing the food import bill. Achieving these targets, however, will require sustained commitment from governments, private sector investment and a generational shift in attitudes toward local food production.

— Source: Adapted from a CARICOM food security briefing

parts.question: List FIVE MAIN points discussed in the article. You MUST use complete sentences.

rubric.value: Caribbean countries import over 60% of their food creating vulnerability to supply disruptions and price changes
Caribbean agriculture has declined as young people abandon farming for other occupations
cheap imported processed foods have replaced traditional crops leading to rising non-communicable diseases
the Caribbean has the resources to achieve food self-sufficiency with proper investment and a change in attitudes
CARICOM has set targets to reduce food imports requiring government commitment and regional cooperation

parts.question: Write a summary of the article in NOT MORE THAN 120 words. Use your own words as far as possible.

rubric.value: CONTENT: covers food insecurity dependence on imports agricultural decline health consequences and potential solutions
LANGUAGE: own words used paraphrasing effective not merely copying
ORGANISATION: continuous prose coherent logical within word limit

The parent of a student at Crest View Secondary School is concerned about the poor state of the school's sports facilities. She has been unable to speak with the Principal directly. She decides to write a letter to the School Board of Management.

Your letter MUST be in continuous prose.

• format the letter correctly
• select relevant and complete information
• organise and express concerns clearly
• use appropriate grammar, sentence structure, vocabulary, spelling and punctuation.
